radiology_reports.technique: Multiple axial sections were taken through chest without I.V contrast injection (HRCT protocol).
radiology_reports.result:
Air trapping with bronchial wall thickening in bilateral lung fields. Extensive areas of fine nodularity forming tree-in-bud pattern involving all lobes of both lungs. A few small areas of consolidation in right middle lobe and inferior lingular segment of left upper lobe and left lower lobe. Mild bronchiectasis in bilateral lower lobes. Normal trachea and major airway. No pleural effusion seen on either side. 

Images of mediastinal window show moderately enlarged aortopulmonary and left hilar lymphnodes. A few sections through upper abdomen are unremarkable. No significant skeletal abnormality.
radiology_reports.conclusion:
Air trapping with bronchial wall thickening and mild cylindrical bronchiectasis in bilateral lower lobes. Clinical correlation is advised to rule out bronchial asthma. Extensive fine nodularity forming tree-in-bud pattern involving all lobes of both lung fields with few smaller areas of consolidation on either side. Mediastinal and left hilar lymphadenopathy. Findings are suggestive of inflammatory process. differential diagnosis includes pulmonary tuberculosis including atypical mycobacterium. Clinical and lab correlation suggested for further evaluation.  
                                                           
This is an electronically generated report and does not require signature.
